intervention: Expert consensus framework separating external work from internal response
comparator: Heterogeneous athlete-monitoring methods
outcome_measure: Internal-load and external-load framework
instrument_protocol: 2016 Doha expert conference synthesis; method identity required
missingness: NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: No quantitative effect estimate; framework documents heterogeneous methods
uncertainty: Consensus evidence; no target-population validation or universal total
limitations: Consensus rather than validation; no youth middle-distance directness; does not support a downstream state score

intervention: Conceptual separation of external training work and internal response
comparator: Earlier and potentially ambiguous training-load definitions
outcome_measure: Two-component internal/external construct
instrument_protocol: Conceptual commentary; family identity must precede comparison
missingness: NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: No quantitative effect estimate
uncertainty: Operational definitions not extracted beyond abstract; not a validation study
limitations: Commentary; not youth-runner specific; external work cannot stand in for internal response and vice versa

population: Participants performing cycling or basketball; exact composition not verified
intervention: Whole-session RPE multiplied using the Foster session-RPE method
comparator: Heart-rate-based training-load method
component_dose: Steady-state or interval cycling and basketball; exact doses not reported in abstract
outcome_measure: Relationship and absolute-score comparison between sRPE and HR-based scores
instrument_protocol: Named sRPE prompt/scale/timing/duration/formula required; global value must not be split across components
time_points: POST_SESSION; exact timing not verified from abstract
missingness: NOT_REPORTED; missing inputs must not be represented as zero
effect_estimate: Relationships were consistent across exercise types but absolute sRPE scores were significantly greater than HR-method scores
uncertainty: No numeric estimate in verified abstract; HR is not a criterion for every stress construct
limitations: Participant detail unavailable from abstract; not youth runners; correlation does not establish numerical interchangeability

sex: BOTH; counts not reported in abstract
intervention: CR10 whole-session RPE and differential leg/breathlessness RPE collected at multiple delays
comparator: Heart-rate criteria and within-session timing comparison
component_dose: Treadmill familiarization plus regular two-week mesocycle
outcome_measure: sRPE; leg RPE; breathlessness RPE; correlations with HR criteria
instrument_protocol: CR10; response at 0 15 and 30 minutes; persist session-end and response timestamps
missingness: NOT_REPORTED; observations from different timing protocols must not be silently pooled
effect_estimate: At 30 versus 0 minutes: sRPE -26.5% +/- 5.5%; leg RPE -20.5% +/- 5.6%; breathlessness -38.9% +/- 7.4%; HR correlations largest at completion r=.74-.89
uncertainty: Small short study; 90% limits reported; all timings judged valid but not numerically identical
limitations: Sex-specific results and maturity absent; 800/1500 composition not reported; timing is part of method identity

population: Athletes across sex age competition level and sport
intervention: Foster modified-CR10 session-RPE method
comparator: Validity reliability and internal-consistency evidence across included studies
outcome_measure: Descriptive validity reliability and factors altering RPE
instrument_protocol: Registered CR10 prompt scale timing reporter duration source/unit and formula required
missingness: HETEROGENEOUS_OR_NOT_REPORTED; missing inputs produce not_computed
effect_estimate: Review reports broad validity reliability and internal consistency while identifying factors that alter RPE
uncertainty: Heterogeneous populations protocols and correlation-based evidence
limitations: English restriction; low youth middle-distance directness; review does not make arbitrary units interchangeable

population: Recreational systematically training athletes across sports
intervention: Borg CR10 and Borg 6-20 whole-session ratings 30 minutes after exercise
comparator: Heart-rate reserve and cross-scale comparison
component_dose: Six randomized 30-minute interval sessions at 50% 75% or 85% peak power
outcome_measure: Cross-scale correlations absolute values and HR-reserve correlations
instrument_protocol: Persist scale ID anchors response timing duration and formula; no raw pooling across scales
missingness: NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: CR10 versus HR reserve r=.76; Borg 6-20 versus HR reserve r=.69; scales correlated r=.90 but absolute values differed
uncertainty: Exact sample size absent from abstract; correlation is not agreement
limitations: Adult or mixed-sport indirectness; controlled protocol; transformation requires separate validation

population: Patients through elite athletes across exercise modes
intervention: Historical development of whole-session rating of perceived exertion
comparator: Evidence across aerobic resistance game and clinical exercise modes
outcome_measure: Average perceived intensity of an entire session
instrument_protocol: Original timing about 30 minutes post-bout; wider timing literature exists; scale/prompt/timing/duration must persist
missingness: VARIED_OR_NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: Defines sRPE as an entire-session internal-response construct and not precise external-load detail
uncertainty: Narrative developer-authored review; timing robustness does not erase direct timing differences
limitations: No validation of component allocation; a whole-session sRPE cannot be fractionally assigned to composite-session leaves

intervention: Individualized TRIMP using each runner's heart-rate/lactate profile
comparator: Average group-weighted TRIMP methods
component_dose: Eight weeks of recorded training; individual treadmill testing supplied thresholds
outcome_measure: Relationships of weekly TRIMPi with physiological and performance change
instrument_protocol: TRIMPi method ID version individual test date thresholds and input lineage required
missingness: NOT_REPORTED; absent individual thresholds make TRIMPi not_computed
effect_estimate: Weekly TRIMPi related to speed at 2 mmol/L r=.87 and 4 mmol/L r=.74 and to 5 km/10 km performance; group-average methods were not significant
uncertainty: Very small sample; associations do not establish method agreement or transfer
individual_response: Individual profiles were explicit inputs; response heterogeneity otherwise not reported
limitations: Adult recreational sample; individual testing required; cannot relabel as generic TRIMP or convert to other arbitrary-unit methods

intervention: Whole-session RPE HR-based TRIMP and IMU/peak-ground-reaction-force cumulative biomechanical load
comparator: Cross-method and cross-session-type comparison
component_dose: Endurance submaximal and interval outdoor sessions
outcome_measure: sRPE TRIMP biomechanical cumulative load and pairwise correlations
instrument_protocol: Persist perceptual physiological and biomechanical method IDs units device/protocol and input lineage
missingness: NOT_REPORTED; missing device or subjective inputs must remain typed
effect_estimate: sRPE distinguished session types while other measures did not; correlations r=.49 sRPE/TRIMP r=.25 sRPE/biomechanical r=.35 biomechanical/TRIMP
uncertainty: Small adult sample; criterion adequacy uncertain; only three session types
limitations: No youth/maturity evidence; arbitrary units are construct-specific and must not be summed into one currency

intervention: Whole-session RPE across explosive-power endurance-strength ergometer and functional sessions
comparator: Banister TRIMP for applicable modalities or total weight lifted by modality
component_dose: 194 effective sessions over 12 months; named session examples include 6 km x 3 ergometer work
outcome_measure: Test-retest reliability and sRPE comparator correlations by modality
instrument_protocol: Persist modality scale timing duration comparator and formula; comparator identity changes by modality
time_points: SESSION_LEVEL; exact collection timing not verified
missingness: Effective sessions reported; missingness otherwise not reported
effect_estimate: Ergometer test-retest correlation/ICC about .758/.755; sRPE-TRIMP r=.811 in 6 km x 3 ergometer versus .258 (95% CI -.111 to .565) in functional training
uncertainty: Rowing indirectness; abstract description of session categories is not fully consistent
individual_response: Modality-specific variation is material; individual distributions not reported
limitations: No youth or running directness; criterion changes by modality; no universal conversion or composite-session reduction

population: Adults spanning Fitzpatrick skin tones 1-6; exact n not in abstract
intervention: Six consumer or research optical heart-rate wearables during rest and activity
comparator: Electrocardiography criterion and between-device/activity comparison
outcome_measure: Optical heart-rate error by device activity and skin tone; device missing values
instrument_protocol: Device model algorithm/firmware when available activity context ECG protocol timestamps and missingness reason required
missingness: Full-text figures analyze device missing values; exact rates not extracted
effect_estimate: No significant accuracy difference by skin tone; significant device/activity differences; mean absolute error during activity about 30% higher than rest
uncertainty: Exact n absent from abstract; device algorithms age quickly; no runner-specific estimate
individual_response: Participant-factor analyses present but individual response not extracted
limitations: Not youth-runner specific; optical HR findings do not transfer to distance elevation or energy; algorithm changes break continuity

intervention: Seven wrist-worn devices during sitting walking running and cycling
comparator: Telemetry for heart rate and indirect calorimetry for energy expenditure
outcome_measure: Heart-rate and energy-expenditure error by device activity and participant factors
instrument_protocol: Persist device/model algorithm activity outcome and criterion protocol separately
missingness: NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: Six devices had median HR error below 5% during cycling; no device had energy-expenditure error below 20%; errors varied by activity/device/person
uncertainty: Adult laboratory study using now-old models; cycling result cannot be assumed for running
individual_response: Participant factors affected error; no individual series extracted
limitations: Vendor energy HR distance and pace are different outcomes and cannot substitute for one another

intervention: Positioning-enabled watches measuring distance across urban forest and track routes
comparator: Reference route distances 404.0-4296.9 m
component_dose: 3 x 12 measurements across activities and environments
outcome_measure: Distance mean absolute percentage error and underestimation
instrument_protocol: Persist device model firmware/algorithm when known environment activity route protocol and unit
missingness: NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: MAPE 3.2%-6.1%; underestimation up to 9%; urban/forest and running generally less accurate than open/other conditions
uncertainty: Small repeated routes; model firmware and environment specific
limitations: Instrument rather than athlete validation; algorithm/model changes form a visible continuity boundary

intervention: Systematic review of multiple Garmin models and measured outcomes
comparator: Study-specific criteria and reliability comparisons
outcome_measure: Steps distance energy expenditure heart rate speed and elevation
instrument_protocol: Registry key must include model outcome protocol and version; brand alone is insufficient
missingness: VARIED_OR_NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: Steps generally performed best; distance poor in three studies; energy expenditure variable; HR low-to-excellent; speed/elevation evidence sparse
uncertainty: Heterogeneous older devices; only four reliability studies; author thresholds are not universal cutoffs
limitations: Adult samples; no brand-level validity conclusion; do not merge outcome series across model or algorithm changes

intervention: Ten single or machine-learning imputation methods and multiple imputation
comparator: Observed values masked for reconstruction; method performance compared
component_dose: Jumps per hour and RPE over one season
outcome_measure: Reconstruction performance under simulated masking and actual missingness
instrument_protocol: Canonical observations remain distinct; derivative stores imputation method version predictors uncertainty and source lineage
missingness: Observed values deliberately masked; actual missingness also analyzed; amount and mechanism determine method choice
effect_estimate: Methods combining session and individual context outperformed team-session mean in some analyses
uncertainty: Basketball jump-count context; simulated missingness may differ from real mechanisms; no universal imputation method
individual_response: Individual context improved some reconstructions but does not make imputed values observed
limitations: Youth but not running; imputed derivative must be excluded from canonical observed totals and never silently replace missing

population: Illustrative American-football wearable data; cohort not verified from abstract
intervention: Methodological examination of wearable missing-data mechanisms and possible analysis methods
comparator: Missing-completely-at-random assumption versus informative equipment failure or noncompliance
outcome_measure: Bias in dashboard averages and accumulated summaries
instrument_protocol: Persist eligible count observed count coverage missing reason device/protocol and any derivative method
missingness: Equipment failure and noncompliance can be informative; simple summaries may be biased
effect_estimate: No single effect estimate extracted
uncertainty: Didactic example; exact cohort and mechanism frequencies not verified
limitations: Not youth runners; analysis guidance does not change canonical truth; missing must not be zero-filled or silently removed from denominators

intervention: PROV-O interoperable provenance ontology
comparator: Heterogeneous source systems and revision chains
outcome_measure: Entities activities agents generation usage derivation attribution revision and time
instrument_protocol: Every derived measure is generated by a versioned activity from identified source entities and attributed to software/user agent
missingness: Missing source lineage leaves derivative provenance incomplete; never backfill identity by assumption
effect_estimate: No sport effect estimate; normative data model
uncertainty: General standard does not define physiological meaning or the TRAINORACLE session graph
limitations: Not sport-science validation; revisions require new lineage rather than silent overwrite

intervention: Critical methodological analysis of acute-to-chronic workload ratios
comparator: Published ACWR definitions calculations and causal interpretations
outcome_measure: Ratio artifacts ambiguity causal overinterpretation and inconsistent direction
instrument_protocol: If explicitly displayed persist numerator/denominator windows coupling weighting units missing-day and zero-denominator behavior; default off
missingness: Missing-day behavior is method-defining and must be explicit
effect_estimate: No accepted risk estimate; commentary rejects training recommendations derived from ACWR injury associations
uncertainty: Commentary does not imply every fully specified descriptive ratio is arithmetically useless
limitations: No zones actions readiness injury-risk meaning or plan clearance; raw compatible numerator and denominator must accompany any requested ratio

population: Hypothetical 1000-player squad based on elite Australian-football distributions
intervention: Coupled rolling acute-to-chronic calculation over four independent seven-day periods
comparator: Uncoupled calculation excluding the acute period from chronic denominator
component_dose: Four independent simulated seven-day high-speed-distance periods
outcome_measure: Spurious correlation and example ratio change
instrument_protocol: Persist coupling window weighting input unit and denominator behavior; coupled and uncoupled are separate methods
missingness: NOT_APPLICABLE
effect_estimate: Coupled correlation r=.52 (95% CI .47-.56) despite independent weeks; uncoupled r=.01 (-.05 to .07); example ratio 1.45 versus 1.71
uncertainty: Simulation with no athlete outcome; D20 reports mostly trivial differences in observed datasets
limitations: Methodological simulation; cannot provide outcome or action meaning; supports explicit method provenance only

intervention: Coupled and uncoupled rolling-average and exponentially weighted ACWR variants
comparator: Between-variant comparison in observed datasets
component_dose: Study-specific load series; doses not reported in abstract
outcome_measure: Effect sizes and correlations among ACWR variants
instrument_protocol: Persist coupling and weighting; observed similarity never erases formula identity
missingness: NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: Coupled-versus-uncoupled effect sizes mostly trivial g=.04-.21; variant correlations r=.88-.99
uncertainty: Small elite non-running context; observed similarity is dataset-specific
limitations: No universal preference for uncoupled method; formulas remain distinct and support no zone or action rule

intervention: Narrative model relating recent load to chronic load and training outcomes
comparator: Prior injury-load literature and proposed ACWR interpretation
outcome_measure: Proposed approximate 0.8-1.3 sweet-spot narrative
instrument_protocol: Any ratio must remain named numeric output; proposed bands are not registry semantics
missingness: NOT_REPORTED
adverse_events: Health outcomes are outside accepted RQ-D product outputs
effect_estimate: Affirmative historical rationale only; no accepted product effect estimate
uncertainty: Narrative not intervention evidence; includes unpublished rugby-league data and is contradicted or unsupported by later sources
limitations: Mostly team-sport indirectness; proposed zones and injury language are excluded from descriptive accounting

intervention: Two-week acute load divided by 4 6 8 10 or 12-week chronic hours or kilometres using multiple variants
comparator: Coupled uncoupled and exponentially weighted methods
outcome_measure: Association of specified ACWR variants with observed running-related injury
instrument_protocol: Persist load unit windows coupling weighting observation cadence and sparse-data handling; no classification
missingness: EWMA estimates sparse; exact missingness not extracted
adverse_events: Observed injury outcome is boundary evidence only and not a descriptive product output
effect_estimate: Higher ACWR associated with lower observed injury probability; coupled/uncoupled similar; EWMA sparse/non-significant
uncertainty: Secondary observational analysis; association is non-causal and method/cadence dependent
limitations: Adult runners; no universal threshold risk meaning or plan action; injury association cannot be promoted into product semantics

population: 482 elite Norwegian U19 football players across 34 teams
intervention: Load management using coupled 7:28 rolling ACWR with target 0.8-1.5
comparator: Usual planning in cluster-randomized teams
outcome_measure: Health-problem prevalence and relative risk
instrument_protocol: Specific coupled 7:28 method and target fully identified; retained only as counterevidence to plan authority
missingness: Incomplete response/adherence reported; exact missingness not extracted
adverse_events: Health endpoint outside RQ-D accepted outputs
effect_estimate: Prevalence difference 1.8 percentage points (-4.1 to 7.7; p=.55); RR 1.01 (.91-1.12); no intervention benefit
uncertainty: Football-specific implementation; incomplete response/adherence; does not test descriptive display utility
limitations: Youth but not running; ACWR bands cannot acquire plan authority from this null intervention

intervention: Weekly >=30% load change and ACWR >=1.5 flags under multiple calculations
comparator: Coupled rolling uncoupled rolling and exponentially weighted ACWR methods
outcome_measure: Proportion of sessions flagged and disagreement among calculations
instrument_protocol: Persist load unit threshold window coupling weighting device/GPS provenance and denominator behavior; default no classification
missingness: NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: Flagged proportions: weekly 33.4% coupled 16.2% uncoupled 25.8% EWMA 18.9%; 43.0% of weekly flags differed from coupled/EWMA classifications
uncertainty: Thresholds predefined rather than validated; adult recreational runners; distance only
limitations: Descriptive differences do not select a correct method; no universal threshold zone or action is permitted

population: General physical-activity populations and wearable-sensor validation settings
intervention: Method guidance for standardized evaluation of wearable-derived physical-activity data
comparator: Criterion or reference method selected for the device output and intended use
component_dose: Validation design is metric and context specific; no universal exposure dose
outcome_measure: Reliability sensitivity validity accuracy precision and context transfer
instrument_protocol: Persist device model placement firmware/algorithm processing metric criterion population activity condition and statistical method
time_points: Repeated and criterion-comparison observations appropriate to each metric
missingness: Missing or changed device method algorithm placement wear protocol or criterion prevents silent longitudinal equivalence
effect_estimate: METHOD_GUIDANCE_ONLY; no athlete effect estimate
uncertainty: Trustworthiness depends on device metric population behavior intensity environment criterion and test conditions
limitations: Methods guidance rather than an athlete intervention; no youth middle-distance validation no cross-device equivalence and no readiness recovery safety or prescription authority

population: Team-sport athletes; mainly adult and elite; mixed sex; not distance runners
intervention: Systematic review of single-item self-report wellbeing measures
comparator: Reported relationships with training-load measures and available measurement-property evidence
component_dose: Single items included constructs such as fatigue sleep quality stress soreness and mood; scales and protocols varied
outcome_measure: Association magnitude and direction between wellbeing items or composites and training load; objective utility
instrument_protocol: Persist item wording construct scale anchors timing reporter language and administration context
missingness: Heterogeneous reporting and incomplete measurement-property evidence; absent items remain missing rather than zero
effect_estimate: Relationships ranged from no association to very large association; objective clinical utility and robust measurement properties remained insufficiently established
uncertainty: Heterogeneous constructs scales analyses populations and load measures limit certainty
individual_response: No universal individual-response or action threshold was established
limitations: Team-sport indirectness and weak or incomplete measurement-property evidence; does not validate one fatigue score readiness inference safety decision or 9.5-day rule

population: US HIPAA covered-entity and business-associate context; not a sport-science population
intervention: De-identification under Expert Determination or Safe Harbor
comparator: Disclosure of identifiable protected health information without satisfying an applicable method
component_dose: Expert Determination requires a qualified expert's very-small-risk determination and documented methods/results; Safe Harbor requires specified identifier removal and no actual knowledge of identifiability
outcome_measure: Method-specific de-identification status residual identifiability and documentation
instrument_protocol: Persist jurisdiction covered-entity status dataset fields recipient purpose release version method expert analysis date and transformations; free text is not exempt
missingness: Missing jurisdiction recipient data-field transformation free-text and risk-context facts prevent a de-identification conclusion
effect_estimate: GUIDANCE_ONLY; Expert Determination targets very small rather than zero identification risk and Safe Harbor remains condition specific
uncertainty: Risk depends on dataset recipient reasonably available information technology and context over time
limitations: US HIPAA guidance is not a universal global privacy rule and does not authorize collection analysis sharing coach access or private-note use; private notes remain zero-signal under the product contract
